name: Affleck-Dine baryogenesis
summary: A scalar field carrying baryon or lepton number (often a SUSY flat direction) is displaced from the origin during inflation. As the field rolls, CP-violating dynamics in the potential produce a coherent baryon (or lepton) number that is later transferred to the SM via field decay. Naturally accommodates large initial asymmetries; the produced eta is highly model-dependent.

addresses_puzzles.note: Especially natural in SUSY where flat directions exist for free; can produce eta ~ 10^{-9} with moderate parameters. Q-ball formation along the trajectory is a generic side effect.
Q-balls can be cosmologically stable and contribute to dark matter; this connects baryon asymmetry to DM origin in some realizations.

new_fields: AD scalar (SUSY flat direction or analogous BSM scalar)
introduces.note: Scalar potential parameters + initial conditions; large model dependence.

history: Affleck and Dine (1985). Detailed Q-ball cosmology developed by Kusenko (1997). Active in SUSY-friendly cosmology contexts; constraints come from cosmology (gravitino problem, Q-ball decay phenomenology) rather than collider physics.

references.citation: Affleck and Dine, 'A new mechanism for baryogenesis', Nucl. Phys. B 249, 361 (1985)
Dine and Kusenko, 'The origin of the matter-antimatter asymmetry', Rev. Mod. Phys. 76, 1 (2003)
